validated_commands.quick_start: uv sync --frozen && uv run pytest -m "not slow" && uv run mypy
notes: uv installable via curl; needs PATH update after install
uv sync --frozen installs exact pinned deps from uv.lock
benchmark fixture comes from pytest-codspeed; DO NOT use -p no:codspeed (breaks benchmark tests)
slow marker deselects long-running tests; benchmark tests are NOT marked slow
uv run pytest -m 'not slow' = 187 passed (benchmark tests included, pass via codspeed fixture)

open_prs.title: Batch BS1770 filter pass in write_all (avoid N sosfilt calls)
open_prs.note: CodSpeed confirmed: 88200Hz +23.6%, 96000Hz +17.55%, 44100/48000Hz +11.5%. Review comment addressed (explanatory comment added above block_filtered/block_unfiltered creation explaining BS1770 vs TruePeak split).

closed_prs.outcome: MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-05-04. square_and_sum() helper via np.einsum. CodSpeed: integrated +45%, momentary/short_term/lra +35-37%. Maintainer: 'Very nice!'
MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-05-03. Fused square-sum to avoid redundant quarter-block read. ~2% improvement on momentary/short_term/lra.
MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-05-02. Added TruePeakProcessor benchmark.
MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-04-30. Incremental running sums: momentary -6.2%, short_term -4.7%, lra -5.7%.
MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-04-29. np.dot for channel-weighted sums.

optimization_backlog.desc: Pre-filter all write_all samples in single FilterGroup call vs per-block. PR #119 submitted and awaiting merge.

BlockProcessor.reset() zeros full MAX_BLOCKS=144000 arrays (e.g. _Zij, _block_weighted_sums) even after short sessions. Only block_data views use the sliced property. Optimization: zero only [:block_index] for the large arrays.
optimization_backlog.impact: No CodSpeed impact (reset not in benchmarks). Real-world: meaningful for users measuring many short clips.
optimization_backlog.feasibility: medium - need to verify all read paths are gated by block_index

optimization_backlog.desc: TESTED 2026-05-02 - NO improvement. Do not retry.
lk_log10() scalar path via math.log10. ~1% only, below CodSpeed threshold.
Vendoring/trimming _upfirdn_apply.pyx. Post-PR#119, TruePeak FIR is ~34% of write_all time. Maintainer exploring in PR #116.
optimization_backlog.impact: Up to 34% of Meter.write (dominant bottleneck after PR#119 merged)

maintainer_instructions: Open to exploring _upfirdn_apply.pyx vendoring on separate branch (PR #116 explores this)
2026-05-04: 'Read and address review comments in #117' - DONE
2026-05-05: 'Add explanatory comment above block_filtered/block_unfiltered in write_all' - DONE
perf_notes: CodSpeed threshold ~10% CPU instruction improvement to register as meaningful.
sosfilt is ~23% of Meter.write time. Batching in write_all: 46 calls -> 1 = 1.85x faster filter step. CodSpeed: +11-23% across all meter benchmarks (PR #119).
FilterGroup handles arbitrary-length arrays (IIR state maintained). Batching is numerically identical to per-block (verified atol=1e-10).
Post-PR#119 profile (48kHz, 1s, 10 runs): TruePeak FIR 34%, sosfilt 14%, reset 10%, _write 6%, processing 6%. TruePeak FIR is now dominant.
TruePeakProcessor uses scipy._upfirdn_apply._apply (Cython). Already compiled. No Python-level speedup possible.

BlockProcessor.reset() zeros FULL 144000-element arrays for _Zij, _block_weighted_sums, _quarter_block_weighted_sums, _block_loudness, _blocks_above_abs/rel_thresh. block_data views already use sliced property (efficient). Others wasted for short sessions.
numpy memory pool recycles same-size allocations: pre-allocated buffers do not help.
np.einsum('ij,ij->i', a, a) = sum-of-squares without temp array. PR #117: +45%/+35-37% CodSpeed.
RunningSum->float: NO improvement (tested 2026-05-02). Do not retry.
CodSpeed simulation mode counts CPU instructions - Python dispatch overhead matters.
DO NOT use -p no:codspeed when running tests - breaks benchmark fixture.

infrastructure_notes.codspeed_threshold: ~10% CPU instruction improvement to register as meaningful
infrastructure_notes.benchmark_coverage: 18 benchmarks: block_processor (4 modes), meter (4 rates), TruePeakProcessor (48kHz 4x, 88.2kHz 2x), filter benchmarks
infrastructure_notes.what_shows_in_codspeed: Algorithmic changes or dispatch elimination. Sub-2% local improvements likely invisible.
